primary_concern: Sepsis with acute kidney injury and coagulopathy
clinical_summary: Elevated lactate (4.2 mmol/L) and leukocytosis (14.2 K/uL) meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection with organ dysfunction. Platelets have trended below 100 K/uL (88-95 K/uL) consistent with SOFA coagulopathy scoring. AKI Stage 1 was documented (creatinine 2.1 mg/dL), progressing to Stage 3 (8.9 mg/dL) though the latter value was excluded as a rapid outlier. Bilirubin elevation (1.4 mg/dL) and anemia (Hgb 9.8 g/dL) suggest multi-organ involvement.

prioritized_risks.explanation: Lactate >2 mmol/L, WBC >12 K/uL, and creatinine elevation meet Sepsis-3 criteria for suspected infection plus SOFA increase ≥2
prioritized_risks.threshold: Suspected infection plus acute SOFA increase ≥2

prioritized_risks.explanation: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48 hours (Stage 1) documented
prioritized_risks.threshold: Creatinine rise ≥0.3 mg/dL in 48h or urine output <0.5 mL/kg/hr for ≥6h

prioritized_risks.explanation: Platelets <100 K/uL meets SOFA coagulopathy scoring criteria
Lactate >2 mmol/L present but vasopressor requirements unknown
prioritized_risks.threshold: Vasopressor-dependent hypotension + lactate >2 mmol/L after fluids

recommended_actions: Initiate sepsis bundle: broad-spectrum antibiotics, source control investigation, and hemodynamic monitoring
Assess urine output and renal replacement therapy needs for AKI Stage 1
Monitor platelet transfusion threshold (<20 K/uL) and coagulation parameters
Recheck creatinine with urgent repeat measurement to confirm AKI progression
Evaluate for occult bleeding or thrombocytopenia etiology
doctor_handoff: Prioritize identifying infectious source and assess volume status. Review need for vasopressors if hypotension develops. Confirm AKI baseline creatinine and evaluate for reversible causes.

disease_progression.observation: Platelets 92 K/uL, AKI Stage 3 excluded as outlier

excluded_outliers.reason: Rapid change detected — Creatinine changed by 6.5 mg/dL within 6.0h (max allowed: 2.0). Probable measurement error or specimen issue. Section 15.2 — rise > 2.0 mg/dL in < 6h.
